title: Trichocentrum lowii
intro: Trichocentrum lowii, commonly known as the Yellow Dendrobium or Yellow Butterfly Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) that is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. Trichocentrum lowii contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Trichocentrum lowii is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Trichocentrum and is a member of the family Orchidaceae. Within the family Orchidaceae, it is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is one of the largest subfamilies of orchids, comprising over 20,000 species. Trichocentrum lowii is native to tropical regions.

Its extracts have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
